signals.headline: Recent Confirmed Data:**
signals.raw:
Recent Confirmed Data:**

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics released March 2026 CPI data showing **headline inflation jumped 0.87% monthly**, with the year-over-year rate reaching 3.29%[1]. Energy prices spiked 10.9% in March alone, led by a 21.2% surge in gasoline prices, accounting for nearly three-quarters of the monthly increase[2]. Oil prices jumped from $71/barrel in February to $103/barre

signals.headline: Contrasting Silver Lining:**
signals.raw:
Contrasting Silver Lining:**

Core CPI stands at 2.6% year-over-year (more benign than headline), and Goldman forecasts core goods inflation will decelerate sharply from 2.7% in March to just 0.6% by December 2026 as tariff effects wane[1]. The bank's base case remains a "gradual return to the Fed's comfort zone" if geopolitical risks don't escalate further[1].

signals.raw: HEADLINE | Headline CPI surged to 3.29% year-over-year in March 2026, driven by Iran war energy spike; Goldman raises December inflation forecast by a full percentage point | YES | HIGH confidence

signals.headline: Market-Moving Implications:**
signals.raw:
Market-Moving Implications:**

Goldman Sachs explicitly raised its December 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast by one full percentage point since the Iran war began, with most attributable to energy[1]. Short-term inflation expectations from consumers jumped sharply: the University of Michigan survey rose one percentage point to 4.8%, while financial market-implied expectations for 2026 headline
